frame.core_mechanism: Return migration and selective emigration bias cross-sectional assimilation upward; Duleep-Regets human-capital investment model predicts inverse entry-earnings/growth relationship and country-of-origin convergence — both inflate expected years-in-US and earnings paths used in lifetime NPV
frame.fiscal_gap: No US fiscal micro for return migration (extends G-LIF-D02 CHCP blocked); build sensitivity bands on expected residence years × selective outflow direction before treating SIPP annual proxy as full lifetime horizon
frame.mexico_role: ACS composition weights only — Mexico/Latin America cells show strongest inverse growth (19.9–28.9 pp per $1k entry earnings) but Mexican 1980s cohort exception flagged (IRC A legalization selection)

parameter_claims.population: 96 country/age/education cells, 1975-80 entry cohort, 1980-1990 census follow
parameter_claims.notes: 10-year growth rate falls 5.8 pp per $1,000 higher entry earnings. Unnamed: earnings growth path maps 1:1 to federal tax base without exit truncation.

parameter_claims.population: same 96 cells, weighted by cell sample size
parameter_claims.notes: Weighted estimate: 9.7 pp opposite change per $1,000 entry earnings — policy-relevant for Mexico-heavy immigration weights.
$1,000 lower entry earnings → +2.9 pp (developed) vs +28.9 pp (LDC) 10-year growth. Direct calibration band for Mexico/Central America NAS <HS cells.

parameter_claims.population: Central and South America including Mexico origin region
parameter_claims.notes: 19.9 pp vs 3.1 pp Europe — skill transferability mechanism. Mexican 1980s exception: low initial + slow growth (IRC A legalization selection bias per Ahmed-Robinson).
Split-sample method yields -0.3917 to -0.4870 — survives sampling-error correction. Cross-section assimilation slope ≠ longitudinal path when attrition present.
1965-69 entrants 65% (1970); 1975-79 50% (1980); 1985-89 41% (1990). Low entry triggers high growth per IHCI model — lifetime NPV must pair level with growth, not entry alone.
R²=0.48 on GDP per adult as % of US. Post-1965 composition shift toward LDC explains much of entry-earnings decline — not innate quality decline.

Adding country dummies: R² gain falls 91%→38% (1955-59) and 91%→22% (1965-69) over 10 years. Origin label matters less for assimilation at year 10 — static ACS origin weights overstate persistent origin fiscal heterogeneity.

parameter_claims.population: 27 source countries × age/education cells, 8 cohort comparisons
parameter_claims.notes: CV of median earnings across origins declines 13-55% within 10 years; native CV mostly rises over same periods — convergence is immigrant-specific, not period artifact.

parameter_claims.population: US immigrants who emigrated 1908-1950 (Warren-Kraly); confirmed recent by Duleep 1994
parameter_claims.notes: ~75% of emigrants leave within first 5 years — key horizon truncator for lifetime NPV. High/low emigration country split shows convergence persists (Tables 5-6) — assimilation convergence not emigration artifact, but fiscal years still shorten.

generators.name: Inverse entry-earnings / assimilation growth band
generators.prompt: For each origin×education_bucket in origin_fiscal_scenario_2023, attach Duleep-Regets inverse elasticity band: unweighted -5.8 pp / weighted -9.7 pp / LDC +28.9 pp per $1,000 lower entry earnings (dp631 Table 1-3). Refuse cross-sectional assimilation slope as lifetime earnings path. Pair low entry with high growth before NAS NPV sign call.
generators.retrodiction: Would have blocked Borjas-style 'slow assimilation' from low entry earnings alone AND Clemens/NAS positive paths that assume flat growth across cohorts — dp631 resolves Chiswick-Borjas contradiction via inverse relationship.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime fiscal models use entry earnings or a single YSM coefficient; no paired {entry level, entry-specific growth} cell.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Cross-sectional assimilation coefficient equals cohort longitudinal growth
Low entry earnings signal low immigrant quality rather than high investment incentive
generators.theory_tested: Inverse entry-growth elasticity shifts Mexico <HS lifetime earnings NPV vs static annual proxy

generators.name: Country-of-origin convergence decay on scenario ledger
generators.prompt: Map dp8628 R² and CV convergence (country-origin explanatory power falls 22-91% over 10 years; cross-origin CV -13 to -55%) onto origin_fiscal_scenario rows. Flag origins where year-0 fiscal heterogeneity (avg_federal_net spread) is assumed permanent. Weight Mexico by convergence-adjusted earnings path, not entry-only snapshot.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced that ACS 2023 origin weights × static avg_federal_net overstate Mexico-vs-Europe fiscal gap at year 10+ residence before any return-migration adjustment.
generators.negative_space: Origin label treated as permanent fiscal modifier; no time-in-US decay of country-of-origin earnings premium.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Country-of-origin dummies at entry predict lifetime fiscal sign
generators.theory_tested: Origin R² decay implies diminishing origin-specific fiscal offset after year 10

generators.name: Return-migration / emigration selection sensitivity grid
generators.prompt: Build 3×3 attrition grid on lifetime NPV horizon: {low, base, high exit rate} × {failure-selective, random, success-selective outflow} × {years 0-5, 5-15, 15+ exit windows}. Anchor base rate: Warren-Kraly 75% emigrants in first 5 years; dp631 high/low emigration cohorts show similar inverse growth (emigration does not kill assimilation signal). No US fiscal micro — manual band only until CHCP acquired.
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged G-LIF-D02 CHCP blocked gap and Dustmann survey §5.9 before treating SIPP annual proxy × 75-year horizon as full residence.
generators.negative_space: Immigrant remains until death; selective return migration absent from NAS/Storesletten generational accounting.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrant remains in host country full horizon
Cross-sectional stock equals cumulative person-years for fiscal ledger
generators.theory_tested: Selective early exit truncates PAYG contribution years more than assimilation-adjusted earnings path adds

data_gaps.notes: Canada longitudinal; no clean US public fiscal attrition micro — sensitivity bands only

generators.name: Skill-transferability vs immigrant-quality trap audit
generators.prompt: Audit lifetime memos equating low entry earnings with low 'immigrant quality' (Borjas cohort-quality frame). Cross-read dp631: entry earnings are poor human-capital stock measure when transferability τM<1 drives investment; dp8628: post-1965 LDC composition + family-reunification chain (Korean 75%→44% native parity) explains entry decline with rising growth. Separate {transferability, investment rate, exit hazard} before education_bucket NPV assignment.
generators.retrodiction: Would have prevented Mexico <HS scalar from inheriting 'declining immigrant quality' narrative while ignoring IHCI fast-growth offset and IRC A Mexican cohort selection exception.
generators.negative_space: Single 'quality' dimension collapses transferability, ability, and selective return into entry wage.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Entry earnings rank orders lifetime fiscal contribution
Family-based admission implies lower human capital stock not lower transferability
generators.theory_tested: Transferability-driven low entry + high growth yields higher lifetime NPV than quality-trap reading at same education bucket

generators.name: Cohort-dummy assimilation methodology falsifier
generators.prompt: For any pooled YSM regression or NAS cell using average assimilation rate: simulate dp8628 bias — recent low-entry cohorts get underestimated growth, high-entry cohorts overestimated when cohort dummies constrain growth. Require separate year-of-entry cohort paths or explicit inverse entry×growth interaction before lifetime NPV aggregation on acs_foreign_born_education_bucket_totals weights.
generators.retrodiction: Would have caught standard dummy-cohort assimilation estimates that dp631/dp8628 prove invalid when entry earnings trend down — directly relevant to building lifetime evidence warehouse assimilation parameters.
generators.negative_space: Pooled assimilation coefficient applied to all entry cohorts regardless of entry-earnings level.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Earnings growth stationary across entry cohorts conditional on YSM
Cohort fixed effects control unobserved quality without interacting entry level
generators.theory_tested: Pooled assimilation rate mis-ranks recent low-entry Mexico-weighted cohort vs cohort-specific IHCI path

theories_tested.theory: Cross-sectional assimilation slope approximates full lifetime US residence earnings path
theories_tested.prediction: Applying observed 10-year inverse-growth elasticity to Mexico rows without exit adjustment does not change sign of lifetime NPV vs flat annual SIPP proxy
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT o.origin_label, o.avg_federal_net, e.weighted_adults FROM origin_fiscal_scenario_2023 o JOIN acs_foreign_born_education_bucket_totals_2023 e ON e.education_bucket='<HS' WHERE o.origin_label IN ('Mexico','Guatemala','Honduras'); -- multiply avg_federal_net by horizon_scenarios {25, 40, 75} × growth_band {1.0, 1.2, 1.5} from dp631 LDC elasticity
theories_tested.falsifier: IHCI growth band (+28.9 pp per $1k low entry) × truncated horizon (75% exit by year 5) flips Mexico <HS cell sign vs 75-year no-exit baseline

theories_tested.theory: Country-of-origin fiscal heterogeneity persists at year 10+ (no convergence on scenario ledger)
theories_tested.prediction: Spread in avg_federal_net across Latin American origins remains ≥ entry-year spread after applying dp8628 R²-decay factor (22-91% reduction in origin explanatory power)
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT origin_label, avg_federal_net, avg_federal_net * 0.5 AS convergence_adjusted_y10 FROM origin_fiscal_scenario_2023 WHERE origin_label IN ('Mexico','El Salvador','Guatemala','India','China') ORDER BY avg_federal_net DESC; -- test whether rank ordering collapses after 50% convergence shrink
theories_tested.falsifier: Convergence-adjusted spread <20% of entry spread while static weights still used in compose_scenario_ledger

theories_tested.theory: Return migration is fiscal-neutral (exit before PAYG phase dominates contribution)
theories_tested.prediction: Early exit rate ≤10% or random selection does not move expected person-years in US by >5% for Mexico-weighted <HS stock
theories_tested.duckdb_test: WITH base AS (SELECT SUM(weighted_adults) AS hs_stock FROM acs_foreign_born_education_bucket_totals_2023 WHERE education_bucket='<HS'), scenarios AS (SELECT unnest(['low','base','high']) AS exit_band, unnest([0.05, 0.25, 0.75]) AS exit_rate_y0_5) SELECT b.hs_stock, s.exit_band, b.hs_stock * (1 - s.exit_rate_y0_5) AS expected_y5_stock FROM base b CROSS JOIN scenarios s; -- compare to origin_fiscal_scenario Mexico weighted_adults share
theories_tested.falsifier: Base-case 25% years 0-5 exit (midpoint of Warren-Kraly 75% emigrant timing) reduces effective <HS person-years >15% vs no-exit NAS horizon

disconfirmation: dp8628 Tables 5-6: earnings convergence by origin persists in high- AND low-emigration country splits — emigration does not explain away assimilation convergence (but still truncates fiscal years)
dp631: high/low emigration cohorts show similar inverse entry-growth correlation — return migration bias on growth estimates modest for assimilation, not for NPV duration
dp631 Mexican 1980s exception: low entry + slow growth (IRC A legalization) breaks monotonic inverse relation — Mexico surge cells need cohort-specific not regional band
Chiswick (1980): re-emigration may be higher for high transferability economic migrants — success-selective exit falsifies failure-only attrition band
Family vs employment-based: family entrants start lower but grow faster; converge with employment-based (dp8628 §II) — admission channel matters for path not just level
extraction_method: pdftotext full-document on 2 IZA PDFs (downloaded from docs.iza.org); frame extends G-LIF-D02 attrition sensitivity band
